findings.description: No-write/no-runtime enforcement is complete and correct. Assembler hard-codes all write/runtime/render/route/fetch/audit/idempotency/lock flags to false, rejects any truthy boundaryClaims (forbidden -> 'cannot claim', unknown -> 'cannot accept unknown claim'), and freezes the result. Validator pins the same flags as expected-false and the policy/module/definition/fixture all mirror them. Genuinely metadata-only; no I/O, rendering, or route creation.
findings.adjudication: Informational confirmation; no code change required.

findings.description: Tenant safety is fail-closed: bound/omitted/completion flags and state_transition collapse to the 'blocked' triplet with a blocked_claim_ref when tenant_id != actor/resource/matter tenant. All identifiers are synthetic (tenant.synthetic.lawfirm, user.synthetic.operator-designer, matter.synthetic.control-plane) and fixture_integrity asserts synthetic_only/deterministic/no real client/matter data/no secrets, all script-verified. Allow and deny triplets are exhaustively gated by assertControlPlaneUiOperatorSurfaceInventoryResult.
findings.adjudication: Informational confirmation; no code change required.

findings.location: contract definition.result_fields / policies RESULT_FIELDS / service.js result object / fixture.result
findings.description: Cross-file consistency holds. RESULT_FIELDS (60) matches the assembler result key set and order; fixture.result reproduces assembler output for fixture.input (order-sensitive JSON.stringify check in the validate script will pass); policy/module/definition arrays (source_micro_phase_ids, subphase id sets, evidence refs, acceptance gates, forbidden claims, fail_closed_on) are mutually equal. All new exports are imported and exercised by tests.
findings.adjudication: Informational confirmation; no code change required.

findings.location: contract subphase_closeouts + definition.unit_titles; fixture.unit_titles; policies unitTitles
findings.description: RP00.P04.M00.S01 and RP00.P04.M01.S01 share the identical title 'UI surface inventory' (duplicated in unit_titles). Internally consistent and not script-checked (the validator binds plan on unit IDs/count, not titles), but the duplicate title reduces traceability between the scope-inventory unit and the contract-draft unit.
findings.adjudication: Duplicate UI surface inventory titles are inherited from the locked source ledger; unit IDs, micro IDs, and source micro-phase IDs remain the authoritative trace keys.

findings.location: contract definition.purpose; README.md added line; acceptance_gate_ids / result flags
findings.description: 'Accessibility' and 'design-consistency' contracts are bound only as acceptance-gate ids and boolean result flags (accessibility_contract_bound, design_consistency_bound); they are not enumerated subphase units (unit_count=5, units are M00.S01 + M01.S01-S04). README/purpose prose calling them 'included units' slightly overstates scope. Consistent with metadata-only intent and the unit-id-based plan check, but confirm the plan does not expect dedicated M01.S05/S06 units before source_micro_phases_completed=true is relied on by RP00.P04.M02.
findings.adjudication: README and contract purpose text were tightened so accessibility/design consistency are described as acceptance gates rather than separate included units.

findings.description: requested_pack_id is CP00-057 (an already-closed pack) while pack_id/planned_pack_id are CP00-058, reconciled by correction_reason 'requested CP00-057 shifted to CP00-058 by live sequential cursor' with deviation_from_plan=false. Consistent with the established live-cursor pattern and enforced by the validator; informational only. Upstream evidence correctly binds the five cp00-057 closeout artifacts.
findings.adjudication: Informational confirmation; no code change required.

findings.description: The script cross-checks definition.forbidden_boundary_suite_claims and fail_closed_on against the policy, but does not assert the fixture's forbidden_boundary_suite_claims / fail_closed_on against the policy (only definition's). The fixture values are present and consistent, so this is a minor coverage gap rather than a defect.
findings.adjudication: RP00 validator now checks fixture forbidden_boundary_suite_claims and fail_closed_on arrays against the policy.

findings.location: weighted ledger entries for RP00.P04.M00.S01/M01.S01-S04; docs/closeout-pack-plan CP00-058 entry; docs/closeout-packs/cp00-058 artifacts
findings.description: Out-of-diff prerequisites the validate script requires but that are not in this diff: (a) weighted-ledger boundaries for the five RP00.P04 subphases with correct sourceMicroPhaseId and terminal flags (terminal for M00.S01 and M01.S04 only); (b) a CP00-058 plan entry with risk_class C, unit_count 5, within_default_risk_range=false, override_reason, and included_units = the five subphase ids; (c) the cp00-058 closeout-pack files (manifest, command-evidence, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection with control_plane_ui_operator_surface_inventory_verified=true and subphase_id RP00.P04.M01.S04). These must exist for closeout to pass; the wiring in the diff is correct, but the artifacts themselves are outside the reviewed diff.
findings.adjudication: Closeout artifacts are generated in this pack and final validation is rerun after evidence creation; weighted ledger and plan bindings are checked by validators.

parsed_review.notes: Read-only review of the CP00-058 diff only. The metadata-only UI Operator Surface Inventory boundary suite is internally consistent and correct across contract, policies, service module/assembler, validators, fixture, tests, and the contract-validation script. No-write/no-runtime, tenant fail-closed, synthetic-only, evidence binding to cp00-057, and plan binding (deviation=false, plan_ref present, unit-id/count enforced) all hold. Allow and deny completion triplets are exhaustively validated and the fixture mirrors assembler output (order-sensitive comparison will pass). No blocking correctness, validation, evidence, no-write, tenant/synthetic, or plan-binding defects were found. Findings are low/informational: a duplicated unit title, accessibility/design-consistency bound as gate flags rather than enumerated units (verify plan does not expect separate M01 subphases), an already-closed requested_pack_id reconciled by the live-cursor correction, a minor fixture-array validation-coverage gap, and out-of-diff prerequisites (weighted ledger, plan CP00-058 entry, cp00-058 closeout artifacts) that must exist for the validate script to pass. Could not execute the validate script or tests in this read-only pass; conclusions are from static cross-file tracing.

boundary_checks.plan_binding: pass - CP00-058 Risk C 5-unit plan binding is enforced across contract, policy, module, fixture, README, and validator checks.
boundary_checks.tenant_fail_closed: pass - same-tenant metadata is required and cross-tenant metadata returns blocked refs without completing source micro phases.
boundary_checks.no_runtime_ui_render: pass - runtime_ui_render_permitted=false and UI render claims are rejected.
boundary_checks.no_external_asset_fetch: pass - external_asset_fetch_permitted=false and external asset fetch claims are rejected.
boundary_checks.no_database_storage_idempotency_lock_aud: pass - all persistence/write flags are false.
boundary_checks.upstream_evidence_binding: pass - CP00-057 evidence refs are required by policy and request.
